backlog.title: QA-Discovered Defect Remediation
backlog.description: Remediate the one Major robustness defect (typed reassembly desync) and three Minor issues (stale README, NFA_MAX_PATTERN test-fidelity gap, dead has_been_queried state) discovered by end-to-end creative QA against the qmk-notifier PRD. The implementation is high quality — all shipped suites pass — but adversarial probing beyond the shipped corpus uncovered these gaps. This phase restores robustness to malformed/abandoned typed commands, corrects documentation drift, closes a test-fidelity gap, and resolves a dead-state code smell.

milestones.title: Typed Reassembly Robustness (Major Issue 1)
milestones.description: Fix the typed-command reassembly desync where a malformed/truncated APPLY_HOST_CONTEXT (count != actual ids) permanently breaks legacy layer/command routing. Implement a watchdog that detects typed-message abandonment and resets the reassembly state, then add adversarial test coverage proving the fix works and legacy routing resumes after malformed input.

tasks.title: Implement typed_awaiting_terminator watchdog in notifier.c
tasks.description: Add a static `typed_awaiting_terminator` flag to the hid_notify() byte loop that detects when all declared typed-literal bytes have been consumed but no ETX terminator follows. When the next byte after typed_literal_remaining reaches 0 is NOT 0x03 (ETX), the typed message is malformed — reset typed_mode, typed_literal_remaining, msg_index, and the flag to restore legacy routing. The flag must be set AFTER the extension block (not before, since the counter transiently hits 0 at msg_index==2 and msg_index==5) and cleared at all existing reset points.

subtasks.title: Add typed_awaiting_terminator flag and watchdog reset logic in notifier.c byte loop
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/003_16d737de7a3e/bugfix/001_9bb28e448bd3/architecture/bug_analysis.md for the full byte-by-byte trace and fix validation. The bug is in notifier.c hid_notify() byte loop (lines ~858-936). The `typed_literal_remaining` counter (declared line ~115, static) is extended by host-supplied APPLY_HOST_CONTEXT.count at lines ~924-926 (clamped to buffer room ~250, NOT to stream bytes). When count > actual ids, it over-consumes the intended ETX (0x03) and subsequent bytes, leaving typed_mode=true permanently. The fix adds a watchdog: a `static bool typed_awaiting_terminator` (declare near line ~115 next to typed_literal_remaining).
2. INPUT: notifier.c byte loop at lines ~858-936. The existing state variables: typed_mode (line ~96), typed_literal_remaining (line ~115), msg_index (static), dropping (static). The extension block at lines ~919-926 where typed_literal_remaining is decremented and extended. The ETX gate at line ~862. The ETX reset block at lines ~889-890. The overflow reset at lines ~934-935. The typed-mode entry at lines ~836-837.
3. LOGIC: Implement per PRD §4.6 / §12 robustness requirement. (A) Declare `static bool typed_awaiting_terminator = false;` near line ~115. (B) At the TOP of the byte loop (after `typed_literal` is computed at line ~858, BEFORE the ETX gate at line ~862), add a check: if `typed_awaiting_terminator && !typed_literal && c != ETX_TERMINATOR[0]`, the typed message is malformed — reset all typed state: `typed_mode = false; typed_literal_remaining = 0; typed_awaiting_terminator = false; msg_index = 0;` and fall through to process the current byte as a potential new message start (do NOT use `continue` or `break`; let the byte be appended normally). (C) In the append branch, AFTER the extension block (after line ~926, inside the `if (typed_literal)` block), add: `if (typed_literal_remaining == 0) { typed_awaiting_terminator = true; }`. This must be AFTER the msg_index==2 and msg_index==5 extension logic so the flag only latches when tlr==0 post-extension. (D) Add `typed_awaiting_terminator = false;` at all three existing reset sites: typed-mode entry (~837), ETX boundary (~890), overflow (~935). Mock: none needed — this is pure logic in the byte loop with no external service dependencies.
4. OUTPUT: Modified notifier.c where a malformed/truncated typed command (count != actual ids) is detected and dropped, typed_mode resets, and the next well-formed legacy focus-change string dispatches correctly via process_full_message(). The compile-time guard at line ~28 (NFA_MAX_PATTERN <= 128) must still compile. The full run_notifier_stub_tests.sh gate (64 host cases) must still pass.
5. DOCS: [Mode A] Update the block comment for typed_literal_remaining (notifier.c ~98-115) to document the new typed_awaiting_terminator flag and the watchdog recovery mechanism. Add a brief inline comment at the new check site explaining 'next byte after literals must be ETX; if not, the typed message is malformed (count/ids mismatch) — reset state and drop the frame'. This rides WITH the work, do not create a separate docs subtask.

tasks.title: Adversarial test coverage for malformed/truncated/abandoned typed commands
tasks.description: Add adversarial test cases to test_notifier_host.c that exercise the typed reassembly watchdog: count/ids mismatches, truncated frames, abandoned multi-report messages, and recovery-after-malformed scenarios. These cases close the gap identified in the PRD where shipped tests only exercise the happy path (count == ids, no 0x03 in payload).

subtasks.title: Write adversarial typed-command test cases in test_notifier_host.c (count/ids mismatch, truncated, abandoned, recovery)
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/003_16d737de7a3e/bugfix/001_9bb28e448bd3/architecture/test_infrastructure.md for the exact insertion points and test helper API. test_notifier_host.c uses the CK(cond, name) macro (increments g_pass/g_fail, prints PASS:/FAIL:), send_typed(cmd_id, args, nargs) for single-report typed commands, and manual hid_notify(rep, 32) for multi-report framing. The file structure is a sequence of braced blocks in main() with banner comments. New adversarial cases go AFTER the (multi-rep) block (ends ~line 405) and BEFORE the final printf/return (~lines 406-408). The stub-compiled notifier.o already runs at NFA_MAX_PATTERN=128.
2. INPUT: The fixed notifier.c from P1.M1.T1.S1 (with typed_awaiting_terminator watchdog). The existing test helpers: CK macro, send_typed(), stub_get_last_response(), stub_get_active_layer(), board state variables (board_cmd_en, board_layer_en, mac_cmd_en, etc.), callback counters (cb_mute_en/dis, cb_layout_en/dis). The existing (multi-rep) block pattern at lines 378-405 for manual multi-report framing.
3. LOGIC: Implement per PRD §4.6/§12 robustness and the Steps to Reproduce in h3.0. Add a new bannered section '===== P1.M1.T2 — ADVERSARIAL typed-command framing (malformed/truncated/abandoned) =====' with these test cases (each a braced block using CK assertions): (A) MALFORMED AHC count/ids mismatch: send [0x81][0x9F][0xF0][0x05][layer=224][flags=0][count=5][id=0x41][0x03 ETX][zeros...] in one 32-byte report via hid_notify(). Assert response[0]==0x00 (no typed dispatch — the malformed frame was dropped). Then immediately send a legacy string that SHOULD match a board command/layer (e.g. 'neovide' or use the existing command map). Assert response[0]==1 (legacy match ack, NOT 0x51 typed marker) and that board on_enable fired (board_cmd_en or board_layer_en changed). This directly reproduces the PRD's reproduction steps. (B) TRUNCATED AHC (no ETX): send an AHC header [0xF0][0x05][224][0][5] with only 1 id and NO ETX in a full 32-byte report, then send a second report that is a legacy string. Assert the legacy string dispatches correctly (response[0]==1, board side effects fired). (C) RECOVERY after malformed: send a malformed AHC (case A), then a well-formed QUERY_INFO via send_typed(NOTIFY_CMD_QUERY_INFO, NULL, 0). Assert response[0]==0x51 (typed marker), response[1]==0x01 (QUERY_INFO echo), response[2]==2 (proto_ver). This proves typed commands still work after a malformed frame is dropped. (D) ABANDONED typed message then well-formed AHC: send a typed AHC with count=5 but only 1 id (no ETX), then send a WELL-FORMED AHC (count matches ids). Assert the well-formed AHC dispatches correctly (response[0]==0x51, response[1]==0x05, response[2]==1 applied, layer set, callbacks fired). Mock: none — the stub harness provides all needed QMK symbols.
4. OUTPUT: Modified test_notifier_host.c with new adversarial test cases. All existing 64 cases must still pass. The new cases must all pass (total > 64). The run_notifier_stub_tests.sh gate must report 0 failures for the host driver.
5. DOCS: [Mode A] Update the test_notifier_host.c file header comment (lines ~18-25) to note the new adversarial section and what it covers (malformed/truncated/abandoned typed commands, count/ids mismatch recovery). This rides WITH the work.

milestones.title: Minor Defects & Test Fidelity Remediation
milestones.description: Remediate the three Minor issues: (1) update stale README test results that falsely report SET_OS tests as broken, (2) add a firmware-budget NFA_MAX_PATTERN=128 fidelity test to close the test-fidelity gap, and (3) document the has_been_queried dead state. Concludes with a changeset-level documentation sync task.

tasks.title: Fix stale README test results block (Issue 2)
tasks.description: Update README.md lines ~540-545 to accurately reflect that all 64 test_notifier_host cases pass (including the four SET_OS blocks), and that the SET_OS 0x03/ETX collision is resolved by the length-aware typed reassembly already shipping in notifier.c.

subtasks.title: Update README.md Overall Test Results block to reflect 64/64 host cases passing
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/003_16d737de7a3e/bugfix/001_9bb28e448bd3/architecture/system_context.md. The README.md 'Overall Test Results' block at lines ~536-548 currently states 'the four SET_OS blocks are currently pending an upstream ETX-collision framing fix in notifier.c' and 'the remaining 57 assertions pass'. This is stale: run_notifier_stub_tests.sh reports 'Total tests run: 64 / passed: 64 / failed: 0'. The length-aware typed reassembly (typed_literal_remaining) already ships and resolves the 0x03/ETX collision. The README also has a 'test_notifier_host (64 cases)' description at lines ~516-522 that lists the test coverage accurately (including SET_OS) — only the results block is wrong.
2. INPUT: README.md current content at lines ~536-548. The verified test result: 64/64 passing (from run_notifier_stub_tests.sh).
3. LOGIC: Implement per PRD §11 acceptance gate and Issue 2 (h3.1). Replace the stale block (lines ~540-545) that says SET_OS is pending a fix with accurate text stating all 64 test_notifier_host cases pass, including the four SET_OS blocks, and that the SET_OS cmd_id 0x03 / ETX terminator collision is resolved by the length-aware typed reassembly in notifier.c. Do NOT change the test coverage description at lines ~516-522 (it is already accurate). Do NOT change the pattern-match corpus results (2023/2023) or dispatch/os results (14/14, 31/31) — those are correct.
4. OUTPUT: Updated README.md where the Overall Test Results block accurately shows test_notifier_host: 64/64 cases passing, no pending fixes. A reader will correctly understand SET_OS is functional.
5. DOCS: This IS the documentation fix — no separate docs subtask needed.

tasks.title: Add firmware-budget NFA matcher fidelity test (Issue 3)
tasks.description: Create a new test suite test_fidelity_nfa128.c compiled with -DNFA_MAX_PATTERN=128 and register it in run_all_tests.sh so the matcher is exercised at the firmware production budget. This closes the fidelity gap where the 9 existing pattern suites run at NFA_MAX_PATTERN=2048 (host/test default) while firmware uses 128.

subtasks.title: Create test_fidelity_nfa128.c and register in run_all_tests.sh
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/003_16d737de7a3e/bugfix/001_9bb28e448bd3/architecture/test_infrastructure.md for the exact insertion points and compile/run patterns. The 9 existing pattern suites in run_all_tests.sh (lines 11-19) compile pattern_match.c directly, so NFA_MAX_PATTERN defaults to 2048 (the #ifndef guard at pattern_match.c:286-291). Only notifier.c #defines it to 128 (notifier.c:14). The new suite must compile with -DNFA_MAX_PATTERN=128 to override the default. The result-grep contract for run_all_tests.sh requires main() to print 'Total tests run:' / 'Tests passed:' / 'Tests failed:'. Do NOT add -DNFA_MAX_PATTERN=128 to the existing 9 suites (test_memory_stress intentionally needs 2048).
2. INPUT: pattern_match.c (628 lines) with NFA_MAX_PATTERN guard at line 286, pattern_match.h (API: pattern_match(), free_parsed_pattern()). The compile-time guard at notifier.c:28-30: `typedef char nfa_budget_check[(NFA_MAX_PATTERN <= 128) ? 1 : -1];` — this only checks the BUDGET, not per-pattern fit. The NEW() macro at pattern_match.c:~365 clamps state allocation to NFA_MAX_STATES (reuses last slot on overflow), so a pattern exceeding the budget silently degrades instead of erroring.
3. LOGIC: Implement per PRD §7.9/§11.1/§11.3 and Issue 3 (h3.2). (A) Create test_fidelity_nfa128.c following the test_pattern_match.c structure: file-scope counters (tests_run, tests_passed, tests_failed), a run_test() helper or simple inline assertions, and a main() that prints 'Total tests run:' / 'Tests passed:' / 'Tests failed:'. Key test cases: (i) A pattern with processed-pattern length at exactly 128 bytes — verify it matches correctly. (ii) A pattern with processed-pattern length at 129+ bytes — verify it is clamped by the NFA state pool (NEW() reuses last slot) and does NOT match incorrectly (or returns a safe no-match). The simplest way to create a long processed pattern is a long literal string or repeated character class. (iii) A normal short pattern (e.g. 'hello') — verify it still matches at the 128 budget (smoke test). (B) Add to run_all_tests.sh compile block (after line 19): `gcc -o test_fidelity_nfa128 test_fidelity_nfa128.c pattern_match.c -I. -DNFA_MAX_PATTERN=128 -std=c99`. (C) Add to run_all_tests.sh run sequence (after line ~104): `run_test 'NFA-128 Fidelity Gate' 'test_fidelity_nfa128'`. Mock: none needed — pattern_match.c is self-contained.
4. OUTPUT: New file test_fidelity_nfa128.c and modified run_all_tests.sh. The new suite must compile cleanly with -Wall and pass all its assertions. The existing 9 suites must remain unaffected (2023/2023). The aggregate total in run_all_tests.sh increases by the new test count.
5. DOCS: [Mode A] No docs update needed for this subtask — the test is internal infrastructure with no user-facing surface. The README test results section (updated by P1.M2.T1.S1 and P1.M2.T4.S1) will reflect the new suite count.

tasks.title: Resolve has_been_queried dead state (Issue 4)
tasks.description: Document the has_been_queried variable as intentionally write-only. It is set to true on the first QUERY_INFO (notifier.c:706) per PRD §4.6 requirement, but never read. The PRD only requires the firmware to SET it (the host enforces 'at most once per board boot'). Rather than changing the wire protocol to expose it (risky for host compatibility), document it as reserved for future host observability.

subtasks.title: Document has_been_queried as intentionally write-only in notifier.c
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/003_16d737de7a3e/bugfix/001_9bb28e448bd3/architecture/findings_and_risks.md. The variable has_been_queried is declared at notifier.c:186 (`static bool has_been_queried = false;`) and set to true at notifier.c:706 (QUERY_INFO handler). It is NEVER read by any code path. The PRD §4.6 requires the firmware to set it; the host enforces 'at most once per board boot'. Exposing it via QUERY_INFO would change the 4-byte response payload to 5 bytes, risking host compatibility — NOT recommended for a Minor issue. The existing comments already mention the §4.6 handshake-timing rule.
2. INPUT: notifier.c line 186 (declaration) and line 706 (assignment). The existing comments reference §4.6.
3. LOGIC: Implement per Issue 4 (h3.3). Update the declaration comment at line 186 to clearly state: 'Write-only by design (PRD §4.6): the host enforces at-most-once-per-boot semantics; this flag is reserved for future firmware-side observability/debugging and is intentionally not exposed in the QUERY_INFO response to preserve wire-protocol compatibility.' Update the assignment comment at line 706 similarly if needed. No functional code change — this is documentation-only.
4. OUTPUT: notifier.c where has_been_queried has a clear, accurate comment explaining its write-only design rationale. No behavior change. All tests still pass.
5. DOCS: This IS the documentation fix — the comment in notifier.c IS the doc. No separate docs subtask needed.

tasks.title: Sync changeset-level documentation
tasks.description: Final changeset-level documentation sweep. Update README.md overview/features sections and any cross-cutting documentation to reflect the complete delta: the typed reassembly watchdog fix, corrected test results, the new NFA-128 fidelity suite, and the has_been_queried documentation. This prevents a coherent set of fixes from shipping with a stale README.

subtasks.title: Update README.md overview and features to reflect all changeset improvements
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/003_16d737de7a3e/bugfix/001_9bb28e448bd3/architecture/system_context.md and findings_and_risks.md. The README.md has several sections that may need updates: the 'Overall Test Results' block (already fixed by P1.M2.T1.S1 for the stale SET_OS claim), the test suite descriptions (lines ~510-525), the features/overview sections, and any robustness/guarantee language. After the typed reassembly watchdog fix, the firmware is now robust to malformed/truncated typed commands per §1.3/§12 — the README should reflect this if it discusses robustness guarantees. The new NFA-128 fidelity suite (P1.M2.T2.S1) adds a 10th suite to run_all_tests.sh — the README's test corpus description should mention it if it lists the suites.
2. INPUT: All changes from P1.M1.T1.S1 (watchdog fix), P1.M1.T2.S1 (adversarial tests), P1.M2.T1.S1 (README test results fix), P1.M2.T2.S1 (NFA-128 fidelity test), P1.M2.T3.S1 (has_been_queried doc). The updated test counts: pattern corpus now includes the NFA-128 fidelity suite; notifier host suite now includes adversarial cases.
3. LOGIC: Review README.md in its entirety (569 lines). Update any cross-cutting documentation that is now stale due to the changeset: (A) If the README mentions the number of pattern-match suites (currently 9), update to reflect the new NFA-128 fidelity suite (10). (B) If the README mentions the number of test_notifier_host cases (currently 64), update to reflect the new adversarial cases added by P1.M1.T2.S1. (C) If the README has a robustness/guarantees section discussing firmware behavior under malformed input, ensure it reflects the new watchdog recovery. (D) Verify the 'Overall Test Results' block is fully consistent (P1.M2.T1.S1 already fixed the SET_OS claim; verify the pattern corpus count matches after adding the fidelity suite). Do NOT restate information already accurately captured by P1.M2.T1.S1.
4. OUTPUT: README.md fully consistent with all changeset changes. No stale claims remain. A reader gets an accurate picture of test coverage, robustness guarantees, and feature status.
5. DOCS: This IS the changeset-level documentation sync (Mode B). It depends on ALL implementing subtasks and runs last.
